evals.name: detects-repeated-tool-call
evals.prompt:
Two-step eval. First run the failure-pattern-detect Stop hook against `transcript-positive.jsonl` (4 identical Bash commands) with session_id=`eval-0-pos`. Then run it AGAIN against `transcript-negative.jsonl` (only 2 identical Bash commands — below the count threshold of 3) with session_id=`eval-0-neg`. The hook MUST log `repeated-tool-call` for the positive run and MUST NOT log it for the negative run.

Invoke step 1:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-positive.jsonl","session_id":"eval-0-pos"}' | bash hooks/Stop-failure-pattern-detect.sh

Invoke step 2:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./transcript-negative.jsonl","session_id":"eval-0-neg"}' | bash hooks/Stop-failure-pattern-detect.sh

Then read `.claude/failure-patterns.log` and save it to `log-output.txt`.
assertions: log-output.txt exists
log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-0-pos AND pattern=repeated-tool-call
log-output.txt contains the literal string 'ls -la' on the same line as the eval-0-pos detection
log-output.txt detection line for eval-0-pos contains a recovery recipe (mentions 'Try a different approach' or 'different command' or similar specific direction)
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-0-neg AND pattern=repeated-tool-call (threshold-boundary check)

evals.name: detects-edit-mismatch
evals.prompt: Pipe a transcript with 2 'old_string not found' errors within 5 turns into the Stop hook. The hook detects `edit-mismatch` and logs it with a recovery recipe pointing at re-Reading the file.
assertions: log-output.txt exists
log-output.txt contains the literal string 'edit-mismatch'
log-output.txt contains 'Re-Read' OR 're-read' (recovery recipe)
log-output.txt mentions the file 'foo.ts'

evals.name: detects-stale-read
evals.prompt: Two-step style: invoke the hook with session_id=`eval-2` against the transcript fixture where Read on `bar.ts` happened at turn 1, Write to `bar.ts` happened at turn 10, and Edit on `bar.ts` happened at turn 12 (no re-Read between turn 10 and turn 12). Hook detects `stale-read` pattern.
assertions: log-output.txt exists
log-output.txt contains the literal string 'stale-read'
log-output.txt mentions the file 'bar.ts'
log-output.txt recovery recipe mentions 'Re-read'
log-output.txt detection line for stale-read references session=eval-2

evals.name: detects-recovery-spiral
evals.prompt:
Two-step eval. First run the Stop hook against `spiral-positive.jsonl` (3 consecutive re-Reads of previously-seen files — at threshold) with session_id=`eval-3-pos`. Then run against `spiral-negative.jsonl` (only 2 consecutive re-Reads — below the threshold of 3) with session_id=`eval-3-neg`. Hook must fire `recovery-spiral` only for the positive run.

Save the resulting `.claude/failure-patterns.log` to `log-output.txt`.
assertions: log-output.txt exists
log-output.txt contains a line where session=eval-3-pos AND pattern=recovery-spiral
log-output.txt detection line for eval-3-pos recovery recipe mentions '/clear' OR 'handoff RECOVER' OR 'fresh session'
log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-3-neg AND pattern=recovery-spiral (boundary check)

evals.name: no-false-positives-on-clean-session
evals.prompt:
Pipe a clean varied transcript (different tools, no repetition, no errors) into the Stop hook with session_id=`eval-4-clean`. The hook must NOT write any pattern entry for this session_id. Other sessions' prior detections (if any) may exist in the log — only this session must be clean.

Invoke:
  echo '{"transcript_path":"./mock-transcript.jsonl","session_id":"eval-4-clean"}' | bash hooks/Stop-failure-pattern-detect.sh

Save the resulting `.claude/failure-patterns.log` to `log-output.txt`.
assertions: log-output.txt does NOT contain any line where session=eval-4-clean (no false positive for this session)

evals.name: regression-composes-with-tier-1-stop-hook
evals.prompt: Starting state: Tier 1's Stop-commit-backstop.sh is already installed in `.claude/settings.json`. Run `/failure-pattern-detector install`. After install, `.claude/settings.json` must have TWO Stop entries — the existing commit-backstop AND the new failure-pattern-detect.
assertions: .claude/hooks/Stop-failure-pattern-detect.sh exists after install
.claude/settings.json hooks.Stop has length 2
settings.json still contains the original Stop-commit-backstop.sh entry
settings.json contains a new entry for Stop-failure-pattern-detect.sh

NOTE: this is a structural-composition test only — it does not verify both hooks fire independently against a real transcript. A behavioral composition test should be added once the Stop hook implementation lands in Task 5.
